text.title: Task Creation Principles Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Task Creation Principles. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: TASK touches exactly one primary component; cross-component TASKs split.
rule.rationale: Multi-component TASKs block parallel scheduling and hide dependency edges.

rule.statement: Acceptance section has ≥1 binary check; 'works correctly' rejected.
TASK names a single owner handle.
rule.rationale: Orphan TASKs rot; explicit ownership forces accountability.

rule.statement: Author runs the four-principle rubric before queueing; failures are fixed first.
rule.rationale: Pre-flight catches violations early; post-flight wastes executor cycles.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-task-creation-principles.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
